title: Love Should Be Fought For
description:
Released: September 16, 2016 
Recorded: 2016 
Venue: Houston Improv (Houston, Texas) 
Genre: Comedy, Spoken Word 
Label: Comedy Central Records 

Damaged Goods is a stand-up comedy album by American comedian Ali Siddiq, released on September 16, 2016, through Comedy Central Records . The album was recorded live at the Houston Improv in Texas . Siddiq, who was named Comedy Central's "#1 Comic to Watch" in 2013, found his voice as a comedian while incarcerated for six years beginning at age 19 . The album features Siddiq's signature storytelling style, transitioning smoothly from jokes about his childhood and getting beaten up by his mom in his sleep to stories about his children and his competitive daughter . Notable bits include his deconstruction of racial stereotypes related to chicken and watermelon, as well as his humorous observation about the stereotype of white people liking trail mix, which demonstrates the arbitrary nature of broad generalizations . The album's title reflects Siddiq's unique perspective and background, and critics have praised the album's highly relatable humor, charming ease, and infectious audience engagement . Siddiq himself has described Damaged Goods as his first official release with Comedy Central, part of a larger discography that includes earlier independent releases such as Talking Loud Saying Something (2010), Enjoy Your Life (2013), Freedom of Speech (2013), and subsequent releases like The Prison Manual (2019) .

title: Me and My Lil’ Brother
description:
Released: August 18, 2013
Genre: Comedy, Spoken Word
Label: SoSouth
Producer: Not specified

Enjoy Your Life is a stand-up comedy album by American comedian Ali Siddiq, released on August 18, 2013, through SoSouth Records . The album was released during a prolific year for Siddiq, who also released Freedom of Speech in 2013 following his 2010 debut Talking Loud Saying Something (Live at the Improv) . Siddiq, who was named Comedy Central's "#1 Comic to Watch" in 2013, incorporates his distinctive storytelling style and life experiences into his material, using wit, thought-provoking punchlines, and perfect comedic timing to engage audiences . The album features personal anecdotes and observational humor, with tracks such as "Me And My Lil' Brother," "Being a Father Is a Thankless Job," "The Riot," and "This Is Why

Enjoy Your Life is a stand-up comedy album by American comedian Ali Siddiq, released on August 18, 2013, through SoSouth Records . The album was released during a prolific year for Siddiq, who also released Freedom of Speech in 2013 following his 2010 debut Talking Loud Saying Something (Live at the Improv) . Siddiq, who was named Comedy Central's "#1 Comic to Watch" in 2013, incorporates his distinctive storytelling style and life experiences into his material, using wit, thought-provoking punchlines, and perfect comedic timing to engage audiences . The album features personal anecdotes and observational humor, with tracks such as "Me And My Lil' Brother," "Being a Father Is a Thankless Job," "The Riot," and "This Is Why

title: Half A Blunt
description:
Released: August 20, 2019 (album) 
Recorded: September 2017 
Venue: Bell County Jail (Belton, Texas) 
Genre: Stand-up comedy, spoken word 
Label: Comedy Central Records 
Producer: Chioke Nassor (director) 

It's Bigger Than These Bars is a stand-up comedy album and television special by American comedian Ali Siddiq, released as an album on August 20, 2019 through Comedy Central Records . The special originally premiered on Comedy Central on February 23, 2018, directed by Chioke Nassor . It was filmed at Bell County Jail in Belton, Texas, and is notable as the first televised special shot inside a jail hosted by and starring a performer who is a former prisoner . Siddiq spent six years in prison and discovered his knack for comedy while incarcerated, bringing a unique perspective to his storytelling . The special was shortlisted for a Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Variety Special (Pre-Recorded) in May 2018

title: Fight With A 29-Year-Old Part 1
description:
Released: May 11, 2026
Genre: Stand-up comedy, spoken word
Recorded: 2026
Label: Self-released (YouTube)
Producer: Ali Siddiq

My Two Sons is a stand-up comedy special by American comedian Ali Siddiq, released on May 11, 2026, exclusively on YouTube . The special marked Siddiq's first major release of 2026, part of a prolific run that included three hour-long specials that year alongside Rugged (June 15, 2025) and Mondays .

The special earned Siddiq a nomination for Outstanding Variety Series or Special at the 2026 NAACP Image Awards, making history as the first independently produced and released YouTube special ever to receive a major award nomination . The nomination placed Siddiq in contention alongside major network and streaming productions, highlighting his independent distribution model's success.

title: I’m Not Handy
description:
Released: June 15, 2026
Recorded: 2026
Genre: Stand-up comedy, spoken word
Length: Approx. 60 minutes 
Label: Self-released (YouTube) 
Producer: Ali Siddiq

Rugged is a stand-up comedy special by American comedian Ali Siddiq, released exclusively on YouTube on June 15, 2025 . It was the second of three hour-long specials Siddiq released in 2025, following My Two Sons (May 11, 2025) and preceding Mondays .

The special has accumulated over 7 million views as of 2026  and ranks among the top 10 most-watched comedy specials of 2025 . Siddiq, crowned by Vulture as "stand-up comedy's most prolific storyteller," developed his distinctive comedic voice while incarcerated for six years, where storytelling was "survival, currency, and community" .

Rugged was filmed and produced independently, continuing Siddiq's direct-to-fan distribution strategy through his YouTube channel, which has garnered over 50 million total views across his specials . The special was part of Siddiq's prolific creative output in 2025 that led to his inclusion in Deadline's "Comics Who Won 2025" list .

Genre: Stand-up comedy, spoken word 
Length: 1 hour 22 minutes 
Label: Helium Records 
Producer: Ali Siddiq, Jeremy Essig, Marc Grossman, Joe Eshenbaugh 

The Domino Effect is a stand-up comedy special by American comedian Ali Siddiq, released on May 19, 2022 . The special was filmed in front of a sold-out hometown crowd in Houston, Texas . It is the first installment in Siddiq's Domino Effect series, which chronicles his life story — from growing up in the Houston projects to selling drugs and getting arrested by the FBI at age 19, leading to a six-year prison sentence where he discovered his comedic voice .

The special was praised by The New York Times as "a genre-defying autobiographical epic" . It has amassed over 14 to 15 million views on YouTube, ranking it in the top 5 most-watched comedy specials of 2022 . It also landed Siddiq on multiple year-end best-of comedy lists .

title: Dude Was Smoking Crack In Jail
description:
Released: May 12, 2024 
Genre: Stand-up comedy, spoken word, documentary 
Length: 77–90 minutes 
Label: Accidental Comedy Productions 
Producer: Matthew Schuler, Jarad Clement, Jay Hawley 

The Domino Effect 3: First Day of School is the third chapter in Ali Siddiq's groundbreaking comedy special series, The Domino Effect . Following the massive success of the first two specials—which have garnered over 21 million views combined—Siddiq continues to captivate audiences with his unique brand of humor and storytelling . The special explores Siddiq's first years of incarceration after being convicted for drug trafficking, taking viewers on a journey through his early days in prison . Using his trademark comedic style, Siddiq blends raw, real-life experiences with his signature wit, finding humor in the most unexpected places while navigating the harsh realities of incarceratio

title: Intro
description:
Released: August 20, 2019
Genre: Stand-up comedy, spoken word
Length: 11 tracks (approx. 40-50 minutes)
Label: Comedy Central Records


The Prison Manual is a stand-up comedy album by American comedian Ali Siddiq, released on August 20, 2019, through Comedy Central Records . The album was released the same year Siddiq was a finalist on NBC's "Bring the Funny" . Siddiq spent six years in prison beginning at age 19 after being arrested by the FBI for drug trafficking and discovered his comedic voice while incarcerated, where storytelling was "survival, currency, and community" . The Prison Manual draws directly from that experience, offering a humorous yet raw look at life behind bars. The album was preceded by Siddiq's acclaimed special It's Bigger Than These Bars (2018), which was filmed inside Bell County Jail and shortlisted for a Primetime Emmy Award. Siddiq was named Comedy Central's "#1 Comic to Watch" in 2013 . Track listing includes: The Beauty Shop, The Difference Between a Prisoner and a Criminal, The Calling Card, The Re-Up, I'm a Veteran Drug Dealer, The Gavel, The Man from Laramie, The Kitchen, Locked Up, Work Your Bid, and The Shank.

title: Duct Taped and K-Rogers Money Laundering
description:
Released: May 16, 2026
Genre: Stand-up comedy, spoken word
Length: 90 minutes
Label: Self-released (YouTube)
Producer: Eric Abrams (director)

Loss is a stand-up comedy special by American comedian Ali Siddiq, released on YouTube on May 16, 2026 . Filmed at the Houston Improv in Siddiq's hometown, the special weaves an interconnected tale of a series of losses the comedian experienced in his late-teenage years . These include a girlfriend who moved away, a grocery-store job he was fired from, the death of his baby sister Ashley, and ultimately his freedom following his arrest by federal agents . The special features Siddiq's signature storytelling style, utilizing a unique stage setup of a straight-back chair and a stool to enhance his narrative . He uses these props to pantomime different scenarios—from being trapped in a car trunk to meeting a girlfriend's protective parents—transforming his posture to inhabit various characters . The special also includes documentary-style appearances from Siddiq's sister Erika R. Devine and friends Anthony Colbert Jr. and Khalif Aikens, who verify the real-life stories he tells on stage . Loss has accumulated over 7 million views on YouTube and was described by Vulture as "a virtuosic display of pacing, choreography, and character work" .

Loss has accumulated over 7 million views on YouTube and was described by Vulture as "a virtuosic display of pacing, choreography, and character work" .

title: Buying Car
description:
Released: May 16, 2026
Genre: Stand-up comedy, spoken word
Length: 90 minutes
Label: Self-released (YouTube)
Producer: Eric Abrams (director)

Loss is a stand-up comedy special by American comedian Ali Siddiq, released on YouTube on May 16, 2026 . Filmed at the Houston Improv in Siddiq's hometown, the special weaves an interconnected tale of a series of losses the comedian experienced in his late-teenage years . These include a girlfriend who moved away, a grocery-store job he was fired from, the death of his baby sister Ashley, and ultimately his freedom following his arrest by federal agents . The special features Siddiq's signature storytelling style, utilizing a unique stage setup of a straight-back chair and a stool to enhance his narrative . He uses these props to pantomime different scenarios—from being trapped in a car trunk to meeting a girlfriend's protective parents—transforming his posture to inhabit various characters . title: Inmate #679346
description:
Released: June 16, 2026
Genre: Stand-up comedy, spoken word
Length: 116 minutes
Label: Self-released (YouTube)
Producer: Matthew Schuler, Jay Hawley, Jarad Clement

The Domino Effect 4: Pins & Needles is the fourth and final chapter in Ali Siddiq's critically acclaimed comedy special series, The Domino Effect, released on YouTube on June 16, 2026. The two-hour special tells the true stories of Siddiq's last few years of incarceration for drug trafficking, how he started doing comedy, and his eventual release from prison. From dealing with the pressures of life behind bars to discovering his passion for stand-up comedy, Siddiq's transformation is an inspiring and hilarious story as he shares how his experiences shaped his perspective on life, laughter, and the power of storytelling. The special was directed by Eric Abrams and has accumulated over 6 million views on YouTube. The Domino Effect series as a whole, spanning all four installments, has garnered over 40 million combined views on YouTube.

title: Everyone Loves Wesley
description:
Released: October 29, 2020 (Netflix special) / October 30, 2020 (audio album)
Genre: Stand-up comedy
Length: 60 minutes (approx.)
Label: Netflix / Comedy Dynamics
Director: Brian Volk-Weiss

Smart & Classy is the debut Netflix stand-up comedy special by legendary comedian, actor, and former talk show host Arsenio Hall, released globally on Netflix on October 29, 2020. The special marks Hall's first televised stand-up concert in his decades-long career. Over the course of the performance, Hall reflects on getting older, changing times and culture, social issues, stand-up in today's political climate, his experiences on Coming to America, winning Celebrity Apprentice, his favorite drug, and even bothersome baby toes. The special was directed by Brian Volk-Weiss, who also executive produced alongside Cisco Henson and Arsenio Hall. On October 30, 2020, Comedy Dynamics released the audio version of Smart & Classy on major streaming services including Spotify, Apple Music, Amazon Music, Pandora, and Sirius XM. The vinyl LP release followed in 2020 through Netflix records. Track listing includes: Love Wesley, Dennis Rodman, Kim Jong-Un, Ambien Nap, Baby Toe, Itty Bitty, Smart and Classy, Tiger Woods, Washington DC, Donald Trump & Bernie Sanders, Ashy Avenger, OJ Simpson, A New Day, Spatula Foot, Oldies Like That, and Social Media.

Over the course of the performance, Hall reflects on getting older, changing times and culture, social issues, stand-up in today's political climate, his experiences on Coming to America, winning Celebrity Apprentice, his favorite drug, and even bothersome baby toes. The special was directed by Brian Volk-Weiss, who also executive produced alongside Cisco Henson and Arsenio Hall. On October 30, 2020, Comedy Dynamics released the audio version of Smart & Classy on major streaming services including Spotify, Apple Music, Amazon Music, Pandora, and Sirius XM. The vinyl LP release followed in 2020 through Netflix records. Track listing includes: Love Wesley, Dennis Rodman, Kim Jong-Un, Ambien Nap, Baby Toe, Itty Bitty, Smart and Classy, Tiger Woods, Washington DC, Donald Trump & Bernie Sanders, Ashy Avenger, OJ Simpson, A New Day, Spatula Foot, Oldies Like That, and Social Media.

title: Daughter's Boyfriend
description:
Released: October 5, 2024
Genre: Stand-up comedy
Label: Hartbeat, SiriusXM (Laugh Out Loud Radio)
Producer: Rhonda R. McCullough, NBW Studios, Hartbeat

Tapes From a King is the first and only solo full-length stand-up album by legendary comedian Bernie Mac, released posthumously. The album is a collection of never-before-heard stand-up material from the early days of Mac's career, originally recorded on VHS tapes for the comedian to review his performances and refine his craft . These remastered recordings provide a unique window into the creative process of a comedy legend . The album was produced by Hartbeat (Kevin Hart's entertainment company) in collaboration with NBW Studios and Rhonda R. McCullough, Mac's wife of 31 years . It premiered exclusively on SiriusXM's Laugh Out Loud Radio on October 5, 2024, what would have been Mac's 66th birthday, before being released widely across streaming platforms in November 2024

title: We're Live
description:
Released: June 10, 2022
Genre: Musical Theatre, Show Tunes
Label: Craft Recordings, Concord Theatricals
Producer: Jason Robert Brown, Jeffrey Lesser

Mr. Saturday Night (Original Broadway Cast Recording) is the cast album from the five-time Tony Award-nominated musical comedy starring Billy Crystal . Crystal reprises the role of Buddy Young Jr., the outrageous comedian he portrayed in the 1992 Columbia Pictures film of the same name, which also marked his directorial debut . The musical features music by three-time Tony Award winner Jason Robert Brown and lyrics by Tony nominee Amanda Green, with a book by Crystal, Lowell Ganz, and Babaloo Mandel . The Broadway production opened in April 2022 and received a Grammy Award nomination for Best Musical Theater Album at the 65th Grammy Awards . The album was released on streaming and digital platforms worldwide on June 10, 2022, followed by a CD release on July 1, 2022

Genre: Musical Theatre, Show Tunes
Label: Craft Recordings, Concord Theatricals
Producer: Jason Robert Brown, Jeffrey Lesser

Mr. Saturday Night (Original Broadway Cast Recording) is the cast album from the five-time Tony Award-nominated musical comedy starring Billy Crystal . Crystal reprises the role of Buddy Young Jr., the outrageous comedian he portrayed in the 1992 Columbia Pictures film of the same name, which also marked his directorial debut . The musical features music by three-time Tony Award winner Jason Robert Brown and lyrics by Tony nominee Amanda Green, with a book by Crystal, Lowell Ganz, and Babaloo Mandel . The Broadway production opened in April 2022 and received a Grammy Award nomination for Best Musical Theater Album at the 65th Grammy Awards . The album was released on streaming and digital platforms worldwide on June 10, 2022, followed by a CD release on July 1, 2022

title: I Hate When That Happens
description:
Released: 1985
Genre: Comedy, Novelty
Label: A&M Records – SP-5096
Producer: Arthur Baker, Bob Tischler

Mahvelous! is a comedy album by American actor and comedian Billy Crystal, released in 1985 on A&M Records . The album features portions of Crystal's stand-up act along with songs built around characters he performed on Saturday Night Live, including his popular Fernando Lamas parody . The lead single, "You Look Marvelous" (written with Paul Shaffer), peaked at No. 58 on the Billboard Hot 100 and No. 27 on the Dance chart, while its music video became a staple on MTV . The album reached No. 65 on the Billboard 200 and earned Crystal a Grammy Award nomination for Best Comedy Recording at the 1986 Grammy Awards . Track listing includes "I Hate When That Happens" featuring Christopher Guest, "Sammy For Africa" featuring Paul Shaffer, and "Buddy Young Jr."

title: Las Vegas
description:
Released: December 6, 2020 
Genre: Stand-up comedy
Length: Approximately 60 minutes
Label: Comedy Dynamics, A Nacelle Company
Producer: Eddie Griffin


E-Niggma is the third Showtime comedy special by veteran comedian, film and television star Eddie Griffin, premiering on Showtime on December 6, 2020 and released as an audio album on June 26, 2020 . The special was filmed at The Sayers Club inside the historic Sahara Hotel and Casino in Las Vegas, offering an intimate glimpse into the 10th year of his nightly residency "The Eddie Griffin Experience" . Coined "The Last of the Great Ones," Griffin embodies a no-holds-barred, unapologetic, wry comedic storytelling style reminiscent of Redd Foxx, George Carlin, and his mentor Richard Pryor . He takes the audience on a hilariously twisted journey that garners side-splitting laughter while simultaneously delivering sociopolitical and hypothetical history lessons . The title reflects Griffin's persona as a comedic conundrum wrapped in an enigma—often imitated, yet never duplicated . Track listing includes: Las Vegas, Real Hoes & Polio Pussy, The Shit, The Trump Show, We Goin to Hell or Jail, White Slaves & Robots, Killer Ned, Love Who You Love, Kids & Grandkids, The Comedy Dick, Born a Winner, Patron Won't Leave You Alone, "We Need to Talk", Uncle James, and Sons & Daughters .

Producer: Eddie Griffin


E-Niggma is the third Showtime comedy special by veteran comedian, film and television star Eddie Griffin, premiering on Showtime on December 6, 2020 and released as an audio album on June 26, 2020 . The special was filmed at The Sayers Club inside the historic Sahara Hotel and Casino in Las Vegas, offering an intimate glimpse into the 10th year of his nightly residency "The Eddie Griffin Experience" . Coined "The Last of the Great Ones," Griffin embodies a no-holds-barred, unapologetic, wry comedic storytelling style reminiscent of Redd Foxx, George Carlin, and his mentor Richard Pryor . He takes the audience on a hilariously twisted journey that garners side-splitting laughter while simultaneously delivering sociopolitical and hypothetical history lessons . The title reflects Griffin's persona as a comedic conundrum wrapped in an enigma—often imitated, yet never duplicated . Track listing includes: Las Vegas, Real Hoes & Polio Pussy, The Shit, The Trump Show, We Goin to Hell or Jail, White Slaves & Robots, Killer Ned, Love Who You Love, Kids & Grandkids, The Comedy Dick, Born a Winner, Patron Won't Leave You Alone, "We Need to Talk", Uncle James, and Sons & Daughters .

title: Celebrity Race
description:

Genre: Stand-up comedy
Length: 90–97 minutes
Label: CodeBlack Entertainment
Producer: Jeff Clanagan, Leslie Small


Freedom of Speech is a stand-up comedy special by American comedian Eddie Griffin, released on DVD on April 8, 2012, with an audio album following on April 17, 2012, through CodeBlack Entertainment and later under LOL Records/800 Pound Gorilla Records . The special was filmed live in front of an audience in Atlanta, GA, and directed by Leslie Small . At the time of its release, Griffin was ranked on Comedy Central's list of the Top 100 Greatest Stand-Up Comedians of All Time . Known for his outrageous personality, keen wit, and in-your-face style, Griffin combines personal experiences with his edgy take on everything from politics to pop culture in this 97-minute performance .

title: Intro Politics
description:
Released: July 27, 1993
Genre: Comedy
Length: 64:05
Label: Epic – EK 53806
Producer: Bob Wachs

Message In The Hat is the debut comedy album by American comedian and actor Eddie Griffin, released on July 27, 1993, through Epic Records . The material was recorded live at the Comedy Store in Hollywood, California, in 1992, with three studio-recorded music tracks included on the album . Griffin's debut was noted for its raw, explicit, and riotously funny stand-up routines, addressing topics including politics, racism, religion, and sexuality . The album includes Griffin's signature routines such as "Intro/Politics," which features an impersonation of Ross Perot, "Star Trek," "Whip My Ass" (his original perspective on the Rodney King incident), as well as the sexually explicit "Pussy Makes The World Go Around" and "My Nuts"

title: Orange Avenger
description:
Released: October 18, 2019 
Genre: Comedy, spoken word
Length: 70–74 minutes
Label: Comedy Dynamics, A Nacelle Company
Producer: Not specified

Undeniable is a stand-up comedy special by American comedian and actor Eddie Griffin. The special debuted on Showtime in February 2018 as Eddie Griffin: Undeniable to largely positive reviews. The audio version of the special was released worldwide on October 18, 2019, through Comedy Dynamics, A Nacelle Company. It was later made available for international release via Vimeo On Demand on August 9, 2018. The 70-minute special reflects on Griffin's long-standing career, incorporating hysterical one-liners and popular impersonations, all while paying tribute to close friends and late comedians Charlie Murphy, Bernie Mac, Dick Gregory, and Richard Pryor. Viewers can expect to learn about Griffin's life experiences and relationships through a series of humorous anecdotes, as he celebrates 30 years of his stand-up comedy career.

title: Presidents' Wives
description:
Released: February 19, 2011 
Genre: Stand-up comedy
Length: 82 minutes 
Label: Comedy Central Records
Producer: Eddie Griffin, Ann Flagella, Barry Katz, Brian Volk-Weiss, Eric Kritzer

You Can Tell 'Em I Said It is a stand-up comedy special by American comedian Eddie Griffin that premiered on Comedy Central on February 19, 2011 . The special was filmed live in Oakland, California, with Griffin delivering an unapologetic and high-energy set covering topics including racial stereotypes, politics, Viagra, Michelle Obama, the Tea Party, and Tiger Woods . On February 22, 2011, Comedy Central Records released an uncensored extended DVD version featuring over 40 minutes of material not aired on television, along with a bonus "Backstage Pass" behind-the-scenes feature . The digital audio album was also released on February 22, 2011, through Comedy Central Records . The special was produced by Eddie Griffin alongside Ann Flagella, Barry Katz, Brian Volk-Weiss, and Eric Kritzer .
album: You Can Tell 'Em I Said It

title: Intro
description:
Released: March 13, 2020
Genre: Stand-up comedy
Length: 65 minutes
Label: Comedy Dynamics, A Nacelle Company
Producer: Brian Volk-Weiss (director)

#doinwhatido is a stand-up comedy special by American comedian Gary Owen, released as a TV special on September 7, 2019, with the audio album following on March 13, 2020, through Comedy Dynamics, A Nacelle Company . The special was filmed at the Charline McCombs Empire Theatre in San Antonio, Texas, and directed by Brian Volk-Weiss . Owen bursts with energy as he recounts stories including his early days as a wrestler and as a military recruit, his 20-year friendship with Kevin Hart, his reaction to his daughter dating, and one of the greatest things he has ever heard at a football game . The special is part of Owen's growing library of groundbreaking stand-up comedy specials, which includes Black Famous, I Got My Associates, I Agree with Myself, True Story, Urban Legend, and Breakin' Out the Park

title: Messy, Public Divorce
description:
Released: April 29, 2022 
Genre: Stand-up comedy
Length: 59 min.
Label: Comedy Dynamics, A Nacelle Company
Producer: Brian Volk-Weiss (director)

Black Famous is a stand-up comedy special by American comedian Gary Owen. The TV special premiered on Showtime on December 31, 2022, with the audio album released on April 29, 2022 through Comedy Dynamics, A Nacelle Company . The special was filmed during a period of significant personal turmoil for Owen, taking place amid the COVID-19 lockdowns and following his divorce from his wife of nearly 20 years, Kenya Duke . Up until this special, Owen hadn't been able to speak publicly about what really happened, making the performance a form of healing through laughter . The title refers to Owen's unique status as "Black America's Favorite White Comic," a title Ebony magazine bestowed upon him in 2011 .

The special was filmed during a period of significant personal turmoil for Owen, taking place amid the COVID-19 lockdowns and following his divorce from his wife of nearly 20 years, Kenya Duke . Up until this special, Owen hadn't been able to speak publicly about what really happened, making the performance a form of healing through laughter . The title refers to Owen's unique status as "Black America's Favorite White Comic," a title Ebony magazine bestowed upon him in 2011 .

title: Intro Next in Line
description:
Released: April 7, 2017 
Genre: Stand-up comedy
Length: 70–75 minutes
Label: RLJ Entertainment
Director: Leslie Small

I Agree with Myself is a stand-up comedy special by American comedian Gary Owen, premiering on Showtime on April 7, 2017 , with the audio album released on April 7, 2017, through RLJ Entertainment. The special was filmed at the Stardome Comedy Club in Birmingham, Alabama. Fearless and happy-go-lucky, Owen flips every accepted, politically correct approach to family, race, gender, and politics in his hilarious comedy special. He covers a wide range of topics, including his experiences as an African American man in the entertainment industry, modern dating, the challenges of being a father and husband, his time as a cast member on the BET reality show The Gary Owen Show, and encounters with celebrities including Shaquille O'Neal and Conor McGregor. The special also features a powerful segment addressing police brutality and racial profiling, where Owen emphasizes the importance of peaceful protest and creativity in sparking change.

title: Lead Singer
description:
Released: July 20, 2018
Genre: Stand-up comedy
Length: 60–62 minutes
Label: 800 Pound Gorilla Records
Producer: Not specified

I Got My Associates is a stand-up comedy special by American comedian Gary Owen, premiering on Showtime on September 8, 2017, with the audio album released on July 20, 2018, through 800 Pound Gorilla Records . The special was filmed at the Stardome in Hoover, Alabama, and marks Owen's third stand-up comedy special for Showtime . The title comes from a phrase Owen would hear convicts say on TV at parole board meetings as an argument that they could find work upon release . In this hour-long special, Owen shares stories ranging from celebrity weddings and bumping into LeBron James in the bathroom to the dichotomy of his hillbilly family and being married to the daughter of a founding member of the Black Panthers . He also discusses being the father of teenagers, serving in the military, gamblers, prisons, and even hand washing . The special is dedicated to Owen's brother Dallas Randall, who died of a heroin overdose in 2015, with a post-credit plea urging those struggling with addiction to call the SAMHSA national helpline

title: Radio Suck Up Intro
description:
Released:  February 17, 2015 
Genre: Stand-up comedy
Length: 120 minutes (TV special); 45 minutes (audio album)
Label: Westwood Entertainment


Upgraded is a stand-up comedy special by American comedian Gary Owen, capturing a live set recorded in front of a sold-out crowd . The TV special was released on June 15, 2012, and is noted for including a special documentary on the making of the program . The audio version of the album was released later on February 17, 2015, through Westwood Entertainment . Packed full of the comic's trademark blend of angry rants and witty observations, this special reveals a more seasoned and confident Owen compared to his earlier work. The special is also known as Gary Owen: Upgraded.

title: Beginning Doing Comedy
description:
Released: May 29, 2007
Genre: Comedy, spoken word
Length: 25 tracks
Producer: George Burns, Larry Wilde

George Burns on Comedy (feat. Larry Wilde) is a comedy album that captures the legendary comedian's timeless wit and humorous reflections on life, show business, and comedy itself. The album features 25 tracks, with George Burns and Larry Wilde collaborating both as producers and performers. Burns, who lived to be 100 and performed professionally for nearly his entire life, brings his signature cigar-in-hand charm and impeccable timing to this recording. Larry Wilde, a noted comedy writer and author of several books on comedy, engages the iconic comedian in a lively exchange of comedy knowledge, personal anecdotes, and the philosophy behind making people laugh. The album serves as both an entertaining stand-up recording and an insightful look into the craft of comedy from one of its greatest masters.
album: George Burns on Comedy (feat. Larry Wilde)

title: Airplanes
description:
Released: April 2, 2021
Genre: Comedy
Length: 24 minutes
Producer: Gerald Kelly

That's Funny is a comedy album by American stand-up comedian Gerald Kelly, released on April 2, 2021 . The album consists of 11 tracks with a total duration of 24 minutes . All tracks on the album were written and produced by Gerald Kelly . The album includes the tracks "Airplanes," "Women Got Game," "We Soft," "Dumb," "Please Kill My Wife," "Atlanta," "Six Fags," "Hood Funeral," "Black Spiderman," "Horror Movies," and "We're Different"

title: A Genie Pops Out
description:
Released: December 6, 2005
Genre: Comedy
Length: 49 minutes 
Label: Image Entertainment 
Producer: Adam Reid

Dirty Jokes is a comedy album by American stand-up comedian Gilbert Gottfried, released on December 6, 2005, as both a CD and DVD . The album was recorded live in New York City and features Gottfried delivering a rapid-fire barrage of old-school, offensive jokes without regard for political correctness . The recording culminates in a 10-minute-plus rendition of "The Aristocrats," the legendary dirty joke that Gottfried famously told at the Friars Club roast of Hugh Hefner three weeks after the September 11 attacks in 2001 . The CD version contains 45 tracks, while the DVD runs approximately 49 minutes . Despite over 50 years as a stand-up comedian, Gottfried never released a proper album of his original material during his lifetime because he feared "using up" his jokes and hurting ticket sales for live shows . Dirty Jokes remained his only commercial comedy release prior to his death in April 2022

title: Party Cheese
description:
Released: November 15, 2024 
Genre: Comedy
Length: 14 tracks 
Label: Clown Jewels / 800 Pound Gorilla Media 
Producer: Dara Gottfried (exec.), Neil Berkeley (curation) 

Still Screaming is the first and only official stand-up comedy album of original material by legendary comedian Gilbert Gottfried, released posthumously on November 15, 2024 . Throughout his over 50-year career, Gottfried resisted releasing a comedy album because he feared it would jeopardize his live ticket sales . His widow, Dara Gottfried, and filmmaker Neil Berkeley curated the album using footage captured for the 2017 documentary Gilbert, selecting the comic's best bits and impressions . The title was suggested by comedy roast specialist Jeff Ross . The album includes a guest appearance by Penn Jillette, who contributed liner notes . A portion of proceeds from sales benefits the Gilbert Gottfried Myotonic Dystrophy Type 2 Research Fund at Strong Memorial Hospital in Rochester, NY, supporting research for the rare condition that led to his death in 2022 . The limited-edition deluxe double vinyl gatefold features rare photos, artwork designed by Grammy-nominated art director Perry Shall, a side etched with Gottfried's own original artwork, and a recreation of a free hotel notepad for the track listing—chosen because Gottfried would steal them while on the road

title: Standing Ovation
description:
Released: September 17, 2024
Genre: Comedy
Length: 12 tracks
Label: Counterfeit Sounds
Producer: Counterfeit Sounds

Howie Mandel Does A Show is a comedy album by Canadian comedian Howie Mandel, released on September 17, 2024, across all major streaming platforms . The album marks Mandel's first comedy album in nearly 40 years, returning to his roots in Toronto where it was recorded . It captures the comedian delivering stand-up about his phobias, family, career, and childhood . The album was produced by Counterfeit Sounds, with Anton Leo stating, "With the recent resurgence in popularity of comedy albums, this project feels especially timely" . The album consists of 12 tracks

title: Intro
description:
Released: April 23, 2019
Genre: Stand-up comedy
Length: 55–56 minutes 
Label: Comedy Dynamics 
Producer: Brian Volk-Weiss (director) 

Howie Mandel Presents Howie Mandel at the Howie Mandel Comedy Club is the first solo stand-up comedy special and album from Howie Mandel in over 20 years . The special was filmed at Howie Mandel's Comedy Club inside the Hard Rock Hotel in Atlantic City, New Jersey, which opened on July 4 of the previous year . Mandel conceived the idea himself, approaching the Hard Rock about taping the opening night to create his first televised stand-up special in two decades . The special was directed by Brian Volk-Weiss, CEO of Comedy Dynamics, and released across multiple platforms including iTunes, Google Play, Amazon, Spotify, Dish, DirecTV, and Comcast . In this hour-long performance, Mandel exercises his mastery of making the mundane hilarious as he tells tales about his 40-year marriage, life, career, and his return to the comedy stage . The album marked Mandel's return to stand-up comedy after years of hosting Deal or No Deal and serving as a judge on America's Got Talent, reminding audiences of his roots as an Emmy-nominated comedian and voice actor
album: Howie Mandel Presents Howie Mandel at the Howie Mandel Comedy Club

title: First Time Funny
description:
Released: July 10, 2001
Recorded: Early 1990s (Analog tape recordings)
Genre: Non-Music, Spoken word, Comedy
Length: 56 minutes
Label: Laugh.com
Producer: Larry Wilde (Interviewer)

Jerry Seinfeld on Comedy is a spoken word album featuring the legendary comedian Jerry Seinfeld in conversation with former comic-turned-author Larry Wilde. The album captures interviews conducted early in Seinfeld's meteoric rise, prior to the height of his mega-hit television series. Unlike a traditional stand-up comedy album, this release focuses deeply on the craft and technique of comedy. Over the course of 20 tracks, Seinfeld discusses his creative process, the importance of timing and rhythm, the logic required to trick an audience into a setup, and the rigorous discipline of joke writing. He describes successful humor as containing "thought" and defines a comedian's work as "an exploration into the self". The raw, unfiltered conversations on this album offer a rare glimpse into the mechanics of Seinfeld's comedic mind, covering his early days in the club circuit, his first appearance on The Tonight Show, and the philosophical approach he takes to his persona and motivation

He describes successful humor as containing "thought" and defines a comedian's work as "an exploration into the self". The raw, unfiltered conversations on this album offer a rare glimpse into the mechanics of Seinfeld's comedic mind, covering his early days in the club circuit, his first appearance on The Tonight Show, and the philosophical approach he takes to his persona and motivation

Producer: Katt Williams, Brian Kavanaugh-Jones

American Hustle is a stand-up comedy album by American comedian Katt Williams, released on June 16, 2007, through Reprise Records and Warner Bros. Records . The album is the audio companion to Williams' 2007 comedy film of the same name, also known as Katt Williams: American Hustle or American Hustle: The Movie, which served as a follow-up to his 2006 HBO special The Pimp Chronicles Pt. I . The film was directed by Brit McAdams and had a limited theatrical release on October 26, 2007, earning critical success and establishing Williams as a mainstream comedian . The album consists of eight tracks recorded live in Chicago, where Williams delivers his signature high-energy, raw comedic style covering topics including street life, relationships, and social commentary

title: Your Star Player
description:
Released: January 29, 2009
Recorded: 2008
Venue: Washington, D.C.
Genre: Comedy
Length: 1:13:38
Label: Warner Bros. Records
Producer: Neal Marshall, Thomas Marshall

It's Pimpin' Pimpin' is the second comedy album by Katt Williams, released on January 29, 2009, via Warner Bros. Records . The album was produced by Neal Marshall and Thomas Marshall, with Gary Binkow, Jeff Golenberg, Katt Williams, and Michael Green serving as executive producers . It was recorded live during Williams' highly successful 100-city "It's Pimpin' Pimpin'" tour . The album peaked at number 174 on the US Billboard 200, number 1 on the Comedy Albums chart, and number 5 on the Heatseekers Albums chart . Track listing includes "Your Star Player," "Date a President," "Ritalin and Steroids," "Poor Little Tink Tink," "Laugh First," "Have Your Team Together," "Franchise Player," "Scared of Rope," "The Vaporizer," and "Finally Got My Motorcycle" .

title: Holla If You Hear Me
description:
Released: November 7, 2006
Recorded: April 15, 2006
Venue: Atlanta Civic Center, Atlanta, Georgia
Genre: Comedy
Length: 54 minutes
Label: Salient Media
Director: Anthony Anderson

The Pimp Chronicles Pt. 1 is the debut stand-up comedy special by American comedian Katt Williams, filmed live at the Atlanta Civic Center on April 15, 2006, and released on DVD on November 7, 2006, through Salient Media . The special was directed by Anthony Anderson and features guest appearances by Snoop Dogg, Lil Jon, and Cam'ron . The CD version of the album was released on January 12, 2010, through Warner Bros. Records . In this performance, Williams showcases his high-energy, streetwise comedic style, delivering raw and unfiltered commentary on topics including self-esteem, weed, haters, kids, drinking with white people, Hollywood, and Michael Jackson . The special marked Williams' first major stand-up release and helped establish him as a mainstream comedy force following his recurring role on the Comedy Central cartoon "The Boondocks," where he voiced the character A Pimp Named Slickback . The DVD includes bonus materials such as "Katt Rollin' Up In His Camaro," alternate takes, and behind-the-scenes footage . The album was reissued in 2010 with a different track listing that features 11 tracks .
album: Katt Williams The Pimp Chronicles Pt. 1

title: Born Out of Darkness
description:
Released: October 13, 2009
Recorded: 2009
Venue: Washington, D.C.
Genre: Stand-up comedy
Length: 77–90 minutes
Label: CodeBlack Entertainment, Jam Entertainment, LOL Records
Director: Juan Medina

Pimpadelic is a stand-up comedy special by American comedian Katt Williams, released on DVD on October 13, 2009, through CodeBlack Entertainment and Jam Entertainment . The special was directed by Juan Medina and features live concert footage recorded in Washington, D.C. . Originally, the DVD was titled Katt Williams: Who You Callin' Crazy before being renamed Pimpadelic . The release includes never-before-seen concert footage, bonus clips, offstage interview material, and rare behind-the-scenes footage from Williams' personal vault, offering fans a more intimate look at the comedian's personal side . The material covers Williams' signature over-the-top pimped-out philosophy, with bits on topics including black malls, pharmaceutical side effects, fancy rims, crazy children, and the pros and cons of marriage . The audio version of the album was released on October 13, 2009, through LOL Records, exclusively licensed to 800 Pound Gorilla Records .

title: Mona's Introduction
description:
Released: 2004 
Venue: The Laff House Comedy Club, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Genre: Comedy
Length: 44:34 (Audio Version),
Label: The Laff House, Collectables Records
Director: Not specified

I'm A Grown Little Man is the debut stand-up comedy album by American comedian Kevin Hart, recorded live at The Laff House Comedy Club in Philadelphia . The Laff House was where Hart honed his craft early in his career, performing under the name "Lil Kev" . The album captures Hart's raw energy and blend of sharp observational humor with personal anecdotes . He delivers his trademark mix of self-deprecating humor and exaggerated storytelling, covering topics such as relationships, dating, family, getting older, and his frustrations with everyday life . The tracks, including "Fighting," "Tough Guy And Fighting," "Dating A White Girl," and "Mama's Boy," showcase his skill at turning personal experiences into universally relatable comedy . The album marked a turning point in Hart's career, helping to catapult him from the Philadelphia comedy circuit into the national spotlight and paving the way for his subsequent success with specials like Seriously Funny, Laugh at My Pain, and Let Me Explain .
album: I'm A Grown Little Man (Live Comedy From The Laff House)

title: Damn Kids
description:
Released: November 23, 2004
Recorded: 2004
Venue: The Laff House (Philadelphia, Pennsylvania)
Genre: Stand-up comedy
Length: 72 minutes (DVD) / 44:34 (CD)
Label: Collectables Records
Director: Shannon Hartman

I'm a Grown Little Man is the debut stand-up comedy album by American comedian Kevin Hart, released on DVD and CD on November 23, 2004 . The special was filmed at The Laff House Comedy Club in Philadelphia, the venue where Hart first performed stand-up in 2002 under the name "Lil Kev" . In this 72-minute performance, Hart delivers his signature high-energy, self-deprecating comedic style, covering topics including relationships, fighting, dating white girls, and his relationship with his mother . The special was one of the highest-rated specials for Comedy Central  and marked Hart's breakthrough into national prominence.

title: The Intro
description:
Released: February 8, 2020
Genre: Comedy, documentary, variety
Length: 62 minutes
Network: Netflix
Director: Tom Stern

Kevin Hart's Guide to Black History is a Netflix variety special starring Kevin Hart, released on February 8, 2020 . Described as a "sketch comedy-meets-stand-up-meets-Sesame Street," the special highlights the fascinating contributions of black history's unsung heroes in an entertaining and educational format . The special features Kevin Hart telling the stories of African-Americans who have made great contributions to American history with help from comedy friends such as Tiffany Haddish . It was created by Brian Volk-Weiss, written by Brian Volk-Weiss and Evan Waite, and produced by Kevin Hart, Dave Becky, and Leland Wigington through The Nacelle Company
album: Kevin Hart's Guide to Black History

title: Intro
description:
Released: January 17, 2012
Recorded: 2011
Venue: Nokia Theatre at L.A. Live, Los Angeles, California
Genre: Stand-up comedy, documentary
Label: CodeBlack Entertainment
Director: Leslie Small (concert), Tim Story (sketch segments)

Kevin Hart: Laugh at My Pain is a stand-up comedy documentary film starring American comedian Kevin Hart, released theatrically on January 17, 2012 . The film features Hart performing a stand-up special at the Nokia Theatre at L.A. Live in Los Angeles, interwoven with documentary-style footage of Hart visiting his hometown of Philadelphia and a scripted comedy sketch . Taraji P. Henson, RuPaul, and Larry King make appearances in the film . The concert portion was directed by Leslie Small, while the "Plastic Cup Boyz" short film sketch was directed by Tim Story . The film was produced by Jeff Clanagan, with Dave Becky serving as executive producer and Hart as co-director and writer . The theatrical version runs approximately 88 minutes and was rated R for sexual content and pervasive language . The DVD and Blu-ray were released on January 17, 2012, through CodeBlack Entertainment, and include special features such as a Larry King interview and a sneak peek of "Think Like A Man" . The album corresponds to Hart's massively successful "Laugh at My Pain" tour, which established him as a mainstream comedy force following his breakthrough with I'm a Grown Little Man (2009) and Seriously Funny (2010) .

title: I'm Happy
description:
Released: July 3, 2026
Recorded: 2026
Venue: Madison Square Garden, New York City
Genre: Stand-up comedy, documentary
Length: 75 minutes
Label: Summit Entertainment, Codeblack Films
Director: Leslie Small, Tim Story

Let Me Explain is a stand-up comedy concert film by American comedian Kevin Hart, released theatrically on July 3, 2026 . The film captures Hart's record-breaking 2012 "Let Me Explain" concert tour, which spanned 10 countries and 80 cities and generated over $32 million in ticket sales . The concert portion was filmed live at Madison Square Garden in New York City and directed by Leslie Small, while sketch segments and the film's narrative interludes were directed by Tim Story . The film was produced by Jeff Clanagan and Blake W. Morrison through Codeblack Films, with Hart executive producing via his Hartbeat Productions banner . Unlike his previous specials, Let Me Explain interweaves documentary-style footage of Hart's European tour experiences and his return to his hometown of Philadelphia with the sold-out Madison Square Garden performance . The DVD and Blu-ray were released on October 8, 2013, including special features such as a "Backstage Pass" behind-the-scenes featurette and a "No, No, No Let Us Explain" outtakes reel . The film was rated R for pervasive language including sexual references .

title: Good Mood (Live From The Laff House)
description:
Released: 2014
Recorded: 2014
Venue: The Laff House, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Genre: Stand-up comedy
Length: 45 minutes
Label: Lightyear Entertainment
Director: Roderick Millwood

Live Comedy From The Laff House is a stand-up comedy special by American comedian Kevin Hart, filmed live at The Laff House comedy club in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania and released on DVD in 2014 . The special captures Hart's raw, high-energy performance at the venue where he first began his comedy career after quitting his job as a shoe salesman. The 45-minute concert features Hart's signature self-deprecating humor and observational comedy, with track titles including "Good Mood," "Babies," "House Work," "Marriage," "Let A Man Be A Man," "Emotional," "Impress Women," "Laugh," "What Women Like," "Knock Out," "Dad Fight," "Ostrich," "I Put Myself In Situations," "Mom," and "Holy Water" . The DVD includes bonus features such as performances by fellow comedians Rich Salter and William "Spank" Horton, billed as "New Face of Comedy" segments . The album was re-released by Lightyear Entertainment across digital streaming platforms and as an audio CD . The album is sometimes titled or cataloged as Live Comedy From The Laff House, Volume I .
album: Live Comedy From The Laff House

Released: 2014
Recorded: 2014
Venue: The Laff House, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Genre: Stand-up comedy
Length: 45 minutes
Label: Lightyear Entertainment
Director: Roderick Millwood

Live Comedy From The Laff House is a stand-up comedy special by American comedian Kevin Hart, filmed live at The Laff House comedy club in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania and released on DVD in 2014 . The special captures Hart's raw, high-energy performance at the venue where he first began his comedy career after quitting his job as a shoe salesman. The 45-minute concert features Hart's signature self-deprecating humor and observational comedy, with track titles including "Good Mood," "Babies," "House Work," "Marriage," "Let A Man Be A Man," "Emotional," "Impress Women," "Laugh," "What Women Like," "Knock Out," "Dad Fight," "Ostrich," "I Put Myself In Situations," "Mom," and "Holy Water" . The DVD includes bonus features such as performances by fellow comedians Rich Salter and William "Spank" Horton, billed as "New Face of Comedy" segments . The album was re-released by Lightyear Entertainment across digital streaming platforms and as an audio CD . The album is sometimes titled or cataloged as Live Comedy From The Laff House, Volume I .
album: Live Comedy From The Laff House

title: My Kids
description:
Released: July 20, 2010 
Recorded: 2010 
Venue: Allen Theater, Cleveland, Ohio 
Genre: Stand-up comedy
Length: 67 minutes 
Label: Codeblack Entertainment, Comedy Central Records, Art & Industry 
Director: Shannon Hartman 
Producer: Michelle Caputo, Jack Vaughn 

Seriously Funny is the second stand-up comedy special by American comedian Kevin Hart, released following his debut I'm a Grown Little Man in 2004 . The special was filmed live at the Allen Theater in Cleveland, Ohio, and originally broadcast on television in 2010 . In this performance, Hart delivers his signature high-energy, self-deprecating comedic style, making jokes about his family life, growing up in school, and Shaquille O'Neal playing with LeBron James at the time . The special was written by Kevin Hart, Harry Ratchford, and Joey Wills, and directed by Shannon Hartman . It was released as both a DVD and CD, with the CD version published under Comedy Central Records . Hart has stated that Seriously Funny is his favorite special because the way he looked in it inspired him to start working out and become healthier

title: Raccoon Problems
description:
Released: October 14, 2017
Recorded: August 30, 2015
Venue: Lincoln Financial Field, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Genre: Stand-up comedy, concert film
Length: 96 minutes
Label: Universal Pictures, Hartbeat Productions
Director: Leslie Small, Tim Story

Kevin Hart: What Now? is the third theatrical stand-up comedy concert film by American comedian Kevin Hart, following Kevin Hart: Laugh at My Pain (2011) and Kevin Hart: Let Me Explain (2013). The film was released in the United States on October 14, 2017. The stand-up performance was filmed live on August 30, 2015, in front of 53,000 people at Philadelphia's Lincoln Financial Field, marking the first time a comedian had ever performed to a sold-out football stadium. A live dress rehearsal for a small, intimate audience was filmed at the stadium the night before on August 29, 2015. The concert portion was directed by Leslie Small, while James Bond-style parody sketch segments featuring Halle Berry and Don Cheadle were directed by Tim Story.

title: Don't Be Afraid to Touch Me
description:
Released: October 10, 2000 
Genre: Spoken Word, Comedy 

Generations is a comedy album by Kevin James, released on October 10, 2000 . The album is listed in the spoken word and comedy genres, though specific track listings and production details are not available . This release came early in James's career, following his stand-up beginnings on the Long Island comedy scene and his discovery at the 1996 Montreal Comedy Festival . He would later gain widespread fame starring as Doug Heffernan on the CBS sitcom The King of Queens, which premiered in 1998 and ran for nine seasons

title: Everything Hurts-Pre-Diabetic
description:
Released: January 23, 2024 
Recorded: 2023
Venue: The Hanover Theatre and Conservatory for the Performing Arts, Worcester, Massachusetts
Genre: Stand-up comedy
Length: 62 minutes
Label: 800 Pound Gorilla
Director: Kevin James

Irregardless is the third stand-up comedy special by American comedian Kevin James, following Sweat the Small Stuff (2001) and Never Don't Give Up (2018), and his first special for Prime Video . The family-friendly special was filmed at The Hanover Theatre in Worcester, Massachusetts, and features James's unfiltered take on parenting, marriage, and getting older . Topics include motivating children to put down their video games, why he doesn't trust technology, how many Tater Tots he can fit in his mouth, and his anxieties about modern medicine . The special was produced by Amazon Studios and Positive Image Video, with executive producers including Jeff Sussman, Anthony Giordano, and Brady Nasfell . The audio album was released on July 23, 2024, through 800 Pound Gorilla Media across all major streaming platforms

title: Get That Picture
description:
Released: May 1, 2020
Recorded: 2019-2020
Genre: Stand-up comedy
Length: 57 minutes
Network: Netflix
Label: 800 Pound Gorilla Records
Director: Andy Fickman

Never Don't Give Up is the second stand-up comedy special by American comedian Kevin James, released globally on Netflix on April 3, 2018, with the audio album released on May 1, 2020 through 800 Pound Gorilla Records . The special was directed by Andy Fickman and filmed in front of a live audience. In this performance, James steps away from his TV persona to deliver his signature brand of physical comedy and observational humor, covering topics such as fatherhood, marriage, getting older, allergies, and the awkward moments of everyday life . The special marked James's return to stand-up after a long hiatus from the stage, following his first comedy album Sweat the Small Stuff from 2001 . The audio album includes 16 tracks and has a total runtime of 57 minutes and 5 seconds .

title: Pizza Surprise
description:
Released: April 23, 2025
Recorded: July 2025
Venue: Hudson Theatre, Manhattan, New York City
Genre: Stand-up comedy
Length: 42–44 minutes
Label: Comedy Central
Director: Paul Miller

Sweat the Small Stuff is the debut stand-up comedy special by American comedian Kevin James. The special was taped live in July 2025 at New York City's Hudson Theatre and marked the first time Comedy Central had devoted an hour of stand-up to a single comedian . It originally aired on Comedy Central on April 23, 2025 . In this performance, James riffs on life's many "royal" pains, including waiting in line with strangers, negotiating with the airport ticket counter clerk, underwear wedgies, boringly slow answering machine messages, and more . The DVD was released on July 22, 2003, through Comedy Central's home video division, featuring bonus material including promos, appearances on "The Daily Show," and Comedy Central Quickies segments featuring Stephen Lynch

title: Humbly Vulnerable
description:
Released: August 17, 2021
Recorded: March 2021
Genre: Stand-up comedy
Length: 27 tracks (approx. 78 minutes)

title: Intro by Larry Wilde
description:
Released: May 2, 2006
Genre: Comedy, spoken word
Length: 47 minutes
Label: Laugh.com
Producer: Larry Wilde

Milton Berle on Comedy is a spoken word album featuring comedy legend Milton Berle in conversation with former comic-turned-author Larry Wilde . The 21-track album captures Berle discussing his seven decades in show business, from his early days as a child actor to his reign as "Mr. Television" during the 1950s . Berle shares insights on the craft of comedy, covering topics including dramatic roles, the difference between comedians and comics, vaudeville in 1926, audience reactions, bad taste, attitude, rhythm, impressionists, sight gags, the basic premises of jokes, ad-libbing, preparation, and the quality of a show . The album was produced by Larry Wilde, who had a long career as a comedian before writing "Great Comedians Talk About Comedy," a book that compiled his interviews with 17 comedy greats including Berle, Woody Allen, Jack Benny, George Burns, and Johnny Carson . The album's total runtime is approximately 47 minutes .

The 21-track album captures Berle discussing his seven decades in show business, from his early days as a child actor to his reign as "Mr. Television" during the 1950s . Berle shares insights on the craft of comedy, covering topics including dramatic roles, the difference between comedians and comics, vaudeville in 1926, audience reactions, bad taste, attitude, rhythm, impressionists, sight gags, the basic premises of jokes, ad-libbing, preparation, and the quality of a show . The album was produced by Larry Wilde, who had a long career as a comedian before writing "Great Comedians Talk About Comedy," a book that compiled his interviews with 17 comedy greats including Berle, Woody Allen, Jack Benny, George Burns, and Johnny Carson . The album's total runtime is approximately 47 minutes .

In this electrifying, high-energy performance, Lawson brings his trademark infectious enthusiasm to the stage, sharing uproarious tales from his comedic journey . title: The Jack Benny Program (2 15 48)
description:
Released:2008
Genre: Classic Stand-up Comedy, Radio
Content Period: 1950s – 1970s
Format: Satellite Radio (SiriusXM channel 104), Streaming App
Type: Limited-run channel


Comedy Classics is a satellite radio channel launched by SiriusXM that honors timeless stand-up from the golden era of comedy . The channel draws on SiriusXM's enormous classic stand-up library to pay tribute to the icons who shaped the landscape and defined comedy for generations.

The programming features iconic moments from legends of the 1950s through the 1970s. The line-up includes comedy heavyweights such as Bob Newhart, Phyllis Diller, Redd Foxx, Bob Hope, Jonathan Winters, Jackie Gleason, Buddy Hackett, The Smothers Brothers, Flip Wilson, and the infamous Friars Club Roasts .

Comedy Classics is a satellite radio channel launched by SiriusXM that honors timeless stand-up from the golden era of comedy . The channel draws on SiriusXM's enormous classic stand-up library to pay tribute to the icons who shaped the landscape and defined comedy for generations.

The programming features iconic moments from legends of the 1950s through the 1970s. The line-up includes comedy heavyweights such as Bob Newhart, Phyllis Diller, Redd Foxx, Bob Hope, Jonathan Winters, Jackie Gleason, Buddy Hackett, The Smothers Brothers, Flip Wilson, and the infamous Friars Club Roasts .

Length: Approximately 55 minutes
Label: Fuel 2000 Records / Varèse Sarabande / Jewel Records


Two of a Kind is a comedy album that pairs two legendary figures of American "blue comedy" — Redd Foxx and Pigmeat Markham . Released on CD in 2009, this compilation brings together classic, risqué material from both comedians, who were pioneers of raunchy, no-holds-barred stand-up long before it became mainstream .

Redd Foxx (born John Elroy Sanford) was best known for his starring role as Fred G. Sanford on the hit television sitcom Sanford and Son (1972–1977). However, prior to his mainstream fame, he built a legendary career recording over 50 "party records" filled with brazen, sexually charged humor. His raspy delivery, impeccable timing, and funky narrative style proved highly influential on comedians of all backgrounds, including Richard Pryor and Eddie Murphy .

title: South of the Border
description:
Released: 2013
Genre: Stand-up Comedy / Non-Music (Blue Comedy) 
Length: Approximately 24 minutes (Side 1: 9:24, Side 2: 14:40) 
Label: Dooto Records 
Producer: Dootsie Williams (presumably, based on Dootone/Dooto Records practices)


Burlesque Humor is a stand-up comedy album by legendary American comedian Redd Foxx. It was released in 1959 on Dooto Records, the label that launched Foxx's recording career . This album is one of the early, quintessential "party records" that established Foxx as the most notorious "blue" comic of his era .

Recorded during the late 1950s, Burlesque Humor captures Foxx's signature raunchy, unfiltered comedic style—a style characterized by its point-blank riffs and brazen discussions of sex and race . At the time, such material broke new ground and was generally banned from white-owned record stores, sold primarily in Black neighborhoods and often kept "under the counter" . These recordings are live performances, capturing the atmosphere of a nightclub during the "chitlin circuit" era, and consist of complete continuous sides rather than segmented tracks .

For President is a comedy album by legendary American comedian Redd Foxx, released in 2004 on CD by Fuel 2000 Records . The album compiles classic "party record" material from Foxx's extensive catalog, produced decades earlier during his career as the most notorious "blue" comic of his era .

The album title is a play on Foxx's presidential aspirations, which serve as a comedic vehicle for his signature style of raunchy, point-blank humor. Foxx's material broke new ground with its brazen discussions of sex and race, and his funky narrative style and raspy delivery proved highly influential on comic talents of all backgrounds — from Richard Pryor to Eddie Murphy .

For President is a comedy album by legendary American comedian Redd Foxx, released in 2004 on CD by Fuel 2000 Records . The album compiles classic "party record" material from Foxx's extensive catalog, produced decades earlier during his career as the most notorious "blue" comic of his era .

The album title is a play on Foxx's presidential aspirations, which serve as a comedic vehicle for his signature style of raunchy, point-blank humor. Foxx's material broke new ground with its brazen discussions of sex and race, and his funky narrative style and raspy delivery proved highly influential on comic talents of all backgrounds — from Richard Pryor to Eddie Murphy .

title: Foxx-A-Delic, Pt. 1
description:
Released: 1968
Recorded: Live at Milton Prell's Aladdin Hotel, Las Vegas, Nevada
Genre: Stand-up Comedy / Non-Music (Blue Comedy)
Length: Approximately 32:29
Label: Loma Records (a division of Warner Bros. Records)


Foxx-A-Delic is a live stand-up comedy album by legendary American comedian Redd Foxx, released in 1968 on the Loma Records label . The album was recorded live at the Aladdin Hotel in Las Vegas, Nevada, before a "well-lubricated" audience and captures Foxx in classic, unfiltered form .

Don't let the title fool you — Foxx-A-Delic has little if anything to do with the era of peace, love, and LSD and instead captures Foxx playful, rude, and just a little acerbic . As "The King of the Party Records," Foxx opens his show by announcing to his fans: "Tonight is one of the highlights of your life, because I'm good! I swear to God and three other white men, I'm good!"  Moments later, he declares: "I talk about sex and being a Negro, and you're not supposed to talk about sex, but hell, Negroes have sex! Didn't nobody draw us!"

title: Stephen Foster
description:
Released: 2008

Length: Approximately 37 minutes
Label: Master Classics Records, Golden Lane


Getting' Down N' Dirty is a comedy album by legendary American comedian Redd Foxx, originally released in 2008 on Master Classics Records . The album was reissued by Golden Lane in 2022 . It is a spoken word comedy album that captures Foxx's signature raunchy "blue comedy" style .

The 20-track album (though some versions list only 19 tracks, excluding the Sanford and Son theme) features Foxx's classic routines and one-liners . The material reflects the raw, unfiltered comedic style that made Foxx one of the most notorious "blue" comics of his day before he achieved mainstream fame as Fred G. Sanford on the hit television sitcom Sanford and Son (1972–1977) .

title: Stephen Foster
description:
Released: 1994 
Recorded: Live nightclub performance circa late 1960s/early 1970s
Genre: Stand-up Comedy / Non-Music (Blue Comedy)
Length: 35:52
Label: Laff Records (original), MVD Audio / MVD Visual (CD reissue)


I Ain't Lied Yet is a live stand-up comedy album by legendary American comedian Redd Foxx, originally released in 1978 on Laff Records . The album captures a prototypical Foxx nightclub act from the late 1960s or early 1970s, showcasing his raw, unfiltered "blue comedy" style before his mainstream fame as Fred Sanford on Sanford and Son .

As with many of Foxx's vintage titles, there is no liner information indicating the exact origins or location of the performance contained within . However, Foxx's usual modus operandi is to weave a variety of tales and yarns with a loose sexual and racial motif threaded throughout . In essence, that was the beauty of his provocative patter—he developed unique forms of delivery and segues to keep his somewhat limited subject matter interesting and perpetually fresh .

title: On the Loose (Side 1)
description:
Released: 1967  
Recorded: Live in performance
Genre: Stand-up Comedy / Non-Music
Length: Approximately 33:50 
Label: Loma Records (a division of Warner Bros. - Seven Arts Records) 


On the Loose: Recorded Live! is a live stand-up comedy album by legendary American comedian Redd Foxx, released in 1967 on the Loma Records label . It was Foxx's second album for a major label under the Warner Bros. umbrella, following The Both Sides of Redd Foxx (1966) .

While his major label debut found Foxx playing it comparatively "nice," On the Loose sees the legendary "blue" comic returning to more familiar, raunchier form . Though still a somewhat cleaner affair than the dozens of adult-themed "party records" Foxx had independently cut for Dooto and MF Records throughout the 1950s and 1960s, this album gives his risqué side freer reign . Foxx delivers his machine-gun style of humor—jumping rapidly from one short, punchy bit to another—without the extended storytelling that marked some of his other work .

title: Confucius Say
description:
Released: 1959

Length: Approximately 30:59
Label: Dooto Records (original), Hallmark (CD reissue)
Catalog Number: DTL 275


Racy Tales is a classic "party album" by legendary American comedian Redd Foxx, released in 1959 on Dooto Records . The album was also released under the alternative title The New Race Track .

Recorded during the early part of Foxx's career—well before his mainstream fame as Fred Sanford on Sanford and Son—this album captures the raw, unfiltered "blue comedy" that established him as one of the most influential figures in stand-up history . Foxx was paid just $25 to record his first album, Laff of the Party, and Racy Tales followed as part of an onslaught of Dooto releases that sold primarily in Black neighborhoods and were often kept "under the counter" in white-owned record stores .

Catalog Number: DTL 275


Racy Tales is a classic "party album" by legendary American comedian Redd Foxx, released in 1959 on Dooto Records . The album was also released under the alternative title The New Race Track .

Recorded during the early part of Foxx's career—well before his mainstream fame as Fred Sanford on Sanford and Son—this album captures the raw, unfiltered "blue comedy" that established him as one of the most influential figures in stand-up history . Foxx was paid just $25 to record his first album, Laff of the Party, and Racy Tales followed as part of an onslaught of Dooto releases that sold primarily in Black neighborhoods and were often kept "under the counter" in white-owned record stores .

title: The Both Sides of Redd Foxx (Side 1)
description:
Released: 1966 
Recorded: 1966, live during a Hollywood nightclub engagement 
Genre: Stand-up Comedy / Non-Music 
Length: 42:37
Label: Loma Records (subsidiary of Reprise/Warner Bros.) 


The Both Sides of Redd Foxx is a live stand-up comedy album by legendary American comedian Redd Foxx. It was his first LP for Loma Records, the short-lived R&B subsidiary of Frank Sinatra's Reprise label, marking Foxx's jump to the "big leagues" after years of recording "adults only" comedy albums for a number of tiny labels since the mid-1950s .

The album was recorded during a Hollywood nightclub engagement and captures Foxx bantering with his audience—unafraid to verbally spar with them when the mood hit him—as he drifts from one topic to another with a playful but jaundiced eye . His material covers everything from his experiences in the Army and race relations to his wife, ugly people, kids, and drinking . The album's back cover famously bore the headline "Adult Comedy!" .

title: Up Against the Wall, Pt. 1
description:
Released: 1969 
Recorded: Live at the Redd Foxx Club, Los Angeles, California 
Genre: Stand-up Comedy / Non-Music 
Length: Approximately 40 minutes 
Label: Warner Bros. - Seven Arts Records 
Catalog Number: WS 1771 


Up Against the Wall is a live stand-up comedy album by legendary American comedian Redd Foxx. The album was recorded at Foxx's own nightclub in Los Angeles and released in 1969 on Warner Bros. - Seven Arts Records .

This album represents a significant transition in Foxx's career. He was signed to Loma Records (the R&B subsidiary of Reprise) in 1966, and when Loma folded in 1968, he was moved to Reprise's parent label, Warner Bros. Records . While the title suggested the new militancy of the late '60s, Up Against the Wall remained true to what fans had come to expect from Foxx—a collection of short bits and one-liners dealing with sex and race in a good-natured but sharp-tongued manner .

title: Eulogy
description:
Released: July 25, 1975 
Recorded: May 26, 1975 
Venue: Latin Casino, Cherry Hill, New Jersey 
Genre: Stand-up Comedy 
Length: 45:07 
Label: Reprise Records (a subsidiary of Warner Bros. Records) 
Producer: David Banks 


...Is It Something I Said? is the fourth official album by legendary American comedian Richard Pryor, and the first he released under a new contract with Warner Bros. Records—a label he would remain with for the rest of his recording career .

The album is most notable for being the recorded debut of Pryor's beloved character "Mudbone"—an ancient, wise-cracking, and seemingly immortal elderly Black storyteller from rural Peoria . The character appears in two parts: "Mudbone – Intro" and the extended "Mudbone – Little Feets," the latter of which famously includes the line "...and it's deep too!" .

title: Hillbilly
description:
Released: September 10, 1976 
Recorded: February 1976 and July 1976 
Venue: The Comedy Store (title track) and Roxy Theatre, West Hollywood, California 
Genre: Stand-up Comedy
Length: 40:23 
Label: Warner Bros. Records 
Producer: David Banks 


Bicentennial Nigger is the sixth album by legendary American comedian Richard Pryor. It was recorded live at two iconic West Hollywood venues: the title track was captured at The Comedy Store in February 1976, while the remainder of the album was recorded at the Roxy Theatre in July 1976 .

The album's release coincided with America's Bicentennial celebration, and Pryor uses the patriotic moment to deliver a searing, hilarious critique of race relations in America . It is often considered one of his most influential recordings and the album where his comedic voice reached its peak power .

title: Here and Now
description:
Released: November 12, 1983 
Recorded: August 9, 1983 
Venue: Saenger Theatre, New Orleans, Louisiana 
Genre: Stand-up Comedy
Length: 52:30 
Label: Warner Bros. Records 
Producer: David Banks 


Here and Now is the final official studio album of stand-up comedy by legendary American comedian Richard Pryor . Like many of his previous releases, the album was accompanied by a concert film of the same name, which Pryor directed himself .

This album marks a significant turning point in Pryor's life and career. It was recorded when he was sober for the first time since the age of 14, following his near-fatal freebasing incident in 1980 . The album showcases Pryor's return to stand-up comedy after a brief hiatus, capturing his unique ability to find profound and biting humor in intensely personal struggles regarding race, relationships, and his own mortality.

Here and Now is the final official studio album of stand-up comedy by legendary American comedian Richard Pryor . Like many of his previous releases, the album was accompanied by a concert film of the same name, which Pryor directed himself .

This album marks a significant turning point in Pryor's life and career. It was recorded when he was sober for the first time since the age of 14, following his near-fatal freebasing incident in 1980 . The album showcases Pryor's return to stand-up comedy after a brief hiatus, capturing his unique ability to find profound and biting humor in intensely personal struggles regarding race, relationships, and his own mortality.

title: Introduction
description:
Released: July 23, 2021 
Recorded: October 29–30, 1973 
Venue: The Comedy Store, West Hollywood, California 
Genre: Stand-up Comedy
Length: 14 tracks (original)
Label: Omnivore Recordings, Stand Up! Records 
Producer: Richard Pryor, Jennifer Lee Pryor, Reggie Collins, Cheryl Pawelski 


Live at the Comedy Store, 1973 is a concert album by legendary American comedian Richard Pryor, capturing his performances during a four-night run at the iconic Comedy Store in West Hollywood . The material was recorded on October 29 and 30, 1973, during what Pryor intended as a workshop to test and refine new comedic material—not for commercial release .

The album captures Pryor during his most transformative period as a comic, when he evolved from a family-friendly performer in the mold of Bill Cosby into the challenging, politically and socially aware comedian of his most famous later albums . Pryor habitually used the Comedy Store to try out new material, and his shows were well-attended by other comics who were drawn to his artistic evolution .

title: Women
description:
Released: March 24, 1982 
Recorded: December 1981 – January 1982 
Venues: Circle Star Theatre (San Carlos, CA) and Hollywood Palladium (Hollywood, CA) 
Genre: Stand-up Comedy
Length: 59:53 
Label: Warner Bros. Records 
Producer: Richard Pryor, Biff Dawes 


Live on the Sunset Strip is the seventeenth album by legendary American comedian Richard Pryor, released alongside the concert film of the same name in 1982 . The film became the most financially lucrative of Pryor's concert films .

The album is notable for Pryor's raw and unflinching discussion of his near-fatal drug addiction, specifically the incident on June 9, 1980, when he caught on fire while freebasing cocaine and suffered third-degree burns over half of his body . Pryor's comedy transformed significantly from his earlier work following this life-altering event, becoming more personal, candid, and vulnerably introspective while still retaining his sharp wit.

Pryor's comedy transformed significantly from his earlier work following this life-altering event, becoming more personal, candid, and vulnerably introspective while still retaining his sharp wit.

title: Super N r
description:
Released: November 1968
Recorded: Spring and summer 1968
Venue: The Troubadour, West Hollywood, California
Genre: Stand-up Comedy
Length: 33:28
Label: Dove/Reprise Records
Producer: Richard Pryor, Robert Marchese


Richard Pryor is the debut album by legendary American comedian Richard Pryor. It was recorded live in 1968 at the legendary Troubadour club in West Hollywood.

The album cover, a collaboration between Pryor, designer Gary Burden, artist Rick Griffin, and photographer Henry Diltz, was a direct parody of the "naked savage" covers often seen on National Geographic magazine. Pryor, clad only in a loincloth, wore a ring in his nose and brandished a primitive-looking bow and arrow. Gary Burden, the designer, later revealed that the cover earned him two letters: "One was a letter from the National Geographic Society's attorneys offering to sue me for defaming their publication. The second letter was a Grammy nomination for the best album cover". The album was later re-issued with a modified cover, replacing the border of leaves with the Statue of Liberty.

title: I Hope I'm Funny
description:
Released: May 1974 
Recorded: Early 1974 
Venue: Don Cornelius' Soul Train nightclub, San Francisco 
Genre: Stand-up Comedy 
Length: 33:38 
Label: Stax Records (Partee imprint) 
Producer: Richard Pryor, Ron de Blasio 


That Nigger's Crazy is the third official album release by legendary American comedian Richard Pryor . It was recorded live at Don Cornelius' Soul Train nightclub in San Francisco in early 1974 . The album's title was derived from a remark Pryor made about himself in the film Wattstax .

Initially released on Stax Records under their "Partee" imprint, the album's success was temporarily disrupted when Stax suddenly closed later in 1974 . Pryor regained control of the master rights and signed with Warner Bros. Records almost immediately. The album was reissued on November 10, 1975, on their Reprise subsidiary, three months after Pryor's fourth album ...Is It Something I Said? was released .

title: No Respect
description:
Released: 1980
Genre: Stand-up Comedy
Length: 19:33
Label: Casablanca Records (1980)
Producer: Estelle Endler 
 

No Respect is the iconic comedy album by legendary American comedian Rodney Dangerfield, released in 1980 on Casablanca Records . The album perfectly captures the frazzled brilliance of a lovable loser who spent decades toiling away in miserable obscurity . It features Dangerfield's signature self-deprecating one-liners and his famous catchphrase, "I don't get no respect!" .

The album opens with the classic line, "No respect at all—when I was a baby, I was breast-fed by my father!" From there, the barbs come sharp and quick, with Dangerfield's sweaty swagger, corrosive New York tone, and quick-draw delivery physically captured on audio . The album showcases his humor about marital troubles, bad luck, and the everyday indignities that earned him his "no respect" persona.
