title: What's In a Name
description:
Released: 1983 
Genre: Stand-up Comedy
Length: 31:00 (Cassette) , 33:37 (CD) 
Label: MCA Special Products / Universal Special Products 
Producer: Rodney Dangerfield
Executive Producer: Joan Dangerfield


What's In a Name is a stand-up comedy album by the legendary American comedian Rodney Dangerfield, known for his self-deprecating humor and signature catchphrase, "I don't get no respect!" .

The album captures Dangerfield's classic comedic style, featuring his signature monologues about marital troubles, bad luck, and the everyday indignities that earned him his "no respect" persona. The title itself is a play on his famous struggle for recognition .

The album captures Dangerfield's classic comedic style, featuring his signature monologues about marital troubles, bad luck, and the everyday indignities that earned him his "no respect" persona. The title itself is a play on his famous struggle for recognition .

title: Big Man
description:
Released: February 24, 2015
Genre: Stand-up Comedy, Audiobook
Length: 41 minutes
Label: Comedy Dynamics


44 and Still Ticking is a stand-up comedy special and audiobook by comedian, actor, and writer Rodney Perry . The title reflects Perry's personal journey as a survivor—he suffered a stroke and underwent tireless rehabilitation to return to the stage, and he discusses this experience in the special .

Coming up through comedy clubs all across America, Rodney had the opportunity to learn the business of funny from some of the best . His first job in Hollywood was serving as writer and warm-up guy for Kym Whitley's show, Oh Drama, on BET . For four seasons, he served as co-host and "man on the street" for the comedy competition show, Bill Bellamy's Who's Got Jokes? .

title: Down Low
description:
Released: February 15, 2015
Genre: Stand-up Comedy
Length: 88 minutes (film), 57 min (TV version) 
Venue: Birmingham, Alabama 
Label/Distributor: Comedy Dynamics 
Producer: Barrett Dungy, Trae Dungy, Donell Nelson 
Director: Trae Dungy 


Nothing But the Truth is a stand-up comedy special by comedian, actor, and writer Rodney Perry, filmed live in Birmingham, Alabama . Perry is best known as Mo'Nique's wisecracking sidekick on BET's The Mo'Nique Show (2009–2011) .

The special showcases Perry's high-energy brand of humor as he examines the highs and lows of life, love, and happiness in an all-out engaging performance . No subject is safe from Perry's good-humored ridicule, from love and marriage to the ups and downs of simply being alive .

title: Charish Events Gender Reveal
description:
Released: February 26, 2021
Genre: Comedy, Spoken Word
Label: BSeenMedia
Producer: Jason Riggs


Prank Answers, Vol. 1 is an album by comedian Rodney Perry, released on February 26, 2021, through BSeenMedia . The album features spoken word comedic tracks where Perry delivers humorous responses in various prank call scenarios . Rodney Perry is also credited as the songwriter for all tracks on the album .

Rodney Perry is a comedian known for his "brazen reality based comedy" and his roles as co-host on BET's The Mo'Nique Show and TV One's Bill Bellamy's Who's Got Jokes?. He also starred as Harold in Tyler Perry's Madea's Big Happy Family (2011) and appeared in Coming 2 America (2021).

title: Couples
description:
Released: 2019
Genre: Stand-up Comedy


Survivor is the 2019 album by American comedian, actor, and writer Rodney Perry. The title is a powerful and direct reference to Perry's personal life, as he has overcome significant health challenges. Perry is a survivor of a stroke that left him without the use of his left side, undergoing tireless rehabilitation to return to the stage . More recently, prior to the album's release, he also survived a battle with COVID-19 .

Known for his "brazen reality based comedy," Perry draws heavily from his personal experiences, including over 20 years of marriage and raising six children (five girls and one boy) . His comedy often explores the "joys and occasional pains of raising a big family" .

Perry is a survivor of a stroke that left him without the use of his left side, undergoing tireless rehabilitation to return to the stage . More recently, prior to the album's release, he also survived a battle with COVID-19 .

Known for his "brazen reality based comedy," Perry draws heavily from his personal experiences, including over 20 years of marriage and raising six children (five girls and one boy) . His comedy often explores the "joys and occasional pains of raising a big family" .

title: Don't Let Steve Cuss
description:
Released: March 17, 2012
Genre: Stand-up Comedy/Documentary
Length: 90 minutes (1 hour 30 minutes)
Label: Codeblack Entertainment, NuOpp Inc.
Distributor: Freestyle Releasing (Theatrical), Visual Entertainment (DVD)
Director: Leslie Small
Producer: Jeff Clanagan


Don't Trip... He Ain't Through With Me Yet! is a stand-up comedy concert film featuring Steve Harvey, one of the "Original Kings of Comedy." In this performance, Harvey notably leaves his signature "blue" (profane) material at home to deliver a routine suitable for a faith-based audience .

The special was filmed live at Phillips Arena in Atlanta, Georgia, in front of a church congregation as part of MegaFest, an annual four-day evangelical event . The performance showcases Harvey's unique and hilarious perspectives on church life, children, African-American culture, families, and everyday situations, all while retaining his signature "keep it real" attitude .
album: Don't Trip... He Ain't Through With Me Yet!

title: Intro
description:
Released: September 23, 1997
Genre: Stand-up Comedy
Length: 62:38 (approximately 1 hour 2 minutes)
Label: Island Black Music
Producer: Rushion McDonald and Steve Harvey
Executive Producers: Hiriam Hicks, Juan Hull, and Ronald Isley


Live - Down South Somewhere is the debut comedy album by Steve Harvey, marking his first release through a record label (earned via a contract with Polygram).

The album captures Harvey's live stand-up performance, showcasing the raw, energetic, and often irreverent style that would later make him one of the "Original Kings of Comedy." It was released during a prolific period in Harvey's career, as he also starred in The Steve Harvey Show, hosted It's Showtime at the Apollo, and released the HBO special One Man around the same time.

title: Intro Augusta, Georgia
description:
Released: December 20, 1997 
Runtime: 57 minutes 
Genre: Stand-up Comedy 
Venue: William B. Bell Auditorium, Augusta, Georgia 
Director: Chuck Vinson 
Distributor: HBO (original broadcast), 20th Century Fox Home Entertainment (DVD release) 


Steve Harvey: One Man is a stand-up comedy television special filmed live before a sold-out crowd in Augusta, Georgia . This performance predates Harvey's involvement in the record-breaking "Kings of Comedy Tour" and Spike Lee's documentary The Original Kings of Comedy .

During the hour-long special, Steve Harvey draws on his childhood experiences and observations about life and human nature . His comedic material covers topics including how black and white people get fired, fishing, and how women can put together the ideal man . The performance is known for its raunchy, no-holds-barred style, featuring graphic language and sexually explicit dialogue .

title: Radio Show Guests
description:
Released: October 28, 2008
Recorded at the New Jersey Performing Arts Center, Newark, New Jersey 
Genre: Stand-up Comedy
Runtime: 106 minutes (1 hour 46 minutes) 
Label/Distributor: CodeBlack Entertainment, Vivendi Entertainment 
Director: Leslie Small 
Language: English 


Still Trippin' is a stand-up comedy concert film by comedian, television host, and actor Steve Harvey. This special marks a return to Harvey's irreverent comedic roots after his previous release, Don't Trip... He Ain't Through with Me Yet (2006), which was noted for being more family-friendly .

In this 90+ minute performance, Harvey takes the stage with his signature high-energy delivery and sharp, painfully honest wit . No topic is off limits as he explores his personal experiences relating to family, his radio show, work, travel, and a trip to Africa . He tackles subjects ranging from polygamists to poor families, demonstrating the comedic timing and versatility that made him one of the "Original Kings of Comedy" .

The special was filmed before a full house at the New Jersey Performing Arts Center in Newark and showcases Harvey's ability to command an audience with humor drawn from everyday life and personal observations .
